characters.name: Ginger Baldwin
characters.description: Ginger BaldwinBiographical InformationAge17AffiliationsBayside HighThe MaxInterestZack Morris(dated briefly)Physical InformationGenderFemaleHeight5'9AppearancesFirst appearanceThe New GirlPortrayed byBridgette Wilson-Sampras  Gingeris a recurring student atBayside High. She is characterized as a stereotypical dumb blonde with a shallow personality. She first appeared in "The New Girl". She is played byBridgette Wilson-Sampras. She's is super sweet, but a little forgetful, and slightly ditzy. She is described some as a bimbo who is overly happy. However, she tries her best at school, and work.

characters.name: Barry
characters.description: BarryBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnly appearanceDancing to the MaxPortrayed byWilliam Lee Houston Barryis a representative from the spirit association sent to verify Bayside's hours. When the students at Bayside learned that, through sabotage from a Valley student, their hours weren't counted and they had to get all 15,000 hours in a single day, Toddman suggested a dance competition, with mandatory participation by all Bayside students, to get the hours. Barry came to the school to observe and make sure all the students were dancing the entire time. At the end of the night, he blew his whistle and certified the results, qualifying them for the spirit competition.

characters.name: Richard Belding
characters.actor_name: Oliver Clark (Good Morning, Miss Blisspilot)Dennis Haskins(series)
characters.description: Mr. Richard BeldingBiographical InformationAge39-43StatusMarriedRelatives'Rod Belding(Brother)Becky Belding(Wife)Zack Belding (Son)Penny Belding(Neice)AffiliationsBayside HighPhysical InformationHair colorBrownEye colorBrownHeight6'1"AppearancesFirst appearanceGood Morning, Miss Blisspilot (as Gerald Belding)Portrayed byOliver Clark (Good Morning, Miss Blisspilot)Dennis Haskins(series)  Richard Beldingwas the principal ofBayside HighSchool and the show's resident authority figure. He is played byDennis Haskins. He's known for his catch phrase "Hey, hey, hey, what is going on here?" as well as his high-pitched laugh. Belding has frequent run-ins with Zack and his friends as either a foe to be outwitted or a friend to turn to for help. Despite being a responsible authority figure, Mr. Belding is unusual in that he genuinely seems to care about what his students, particularly Zack, think of him.

characters.name: Violet Anne Bickerstaff
characters.description: Violet Anne BickerstaffBiographical InformationAge16StatusAliveRelatives'Mom & DadAffiliationsBayside HighInterestScreech PowersPhysical InformationHair colorBlondeEye colorGreenHeight5'8"AppearancesFirst appearanceHouse PartyPortrayed byTori Spelling Violet Anne Bickerstaffwas Screech's girlfriend. Previously she dated a nerd who treated her poorly until Screech convinced Violet to tell him off. Later, during a dinner date with Violet and her parents, Screech's nervousness leads to a clumsy date, and her parents demand she stop seeing him. This finally is forgiven when Screech convinces Violet to save the school's floundering Glee Club.

characters.name: Heather Brooks
characters.description: Heather Brookswas a girl who attendedBayside High. Zack Morris fell for her, but was shocked to discover that she already had a boyfriend. She was played by actress Christine Taylor.

characters.name: Spencer Buckley
characters.description: Spencer BuckleyBiographical InformationAge14-17 (Season 1)15-18 (Season 2)StatusAliveAffiliationsBayside HighPhysical InformationGenderMaleHair colorBlackEye colorBrownAppearancesFirst appearanceClubs and CliquesLast appearanceLet the Games BeginPortrayed byAbraham Rodriguez Spencer Buckleyis a student atBayside High. At the end of the pandemic, Spencer and all the other students returned to school. Spencer was excited about Jamie and Lexi seeing each other again after dating throughout the pandemic, because they hadn't kissed since their first kiss because of the pandemic. When she saw how excited Spencer was, Lexi asked him to be less excited because it was making her less excited. Later, when they had their second first kiss, Spencer popped up from inside a nearby trashcan to celebrate. They both told him to leave.[1]

Mr Carosi is a tough minded leader but also has a soft spot for those around them once he gets to know them. characters.name: Colt Cassidy
characters.description: Colt CassidyBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesFirst appearancePilotLast appearanceLet the Games BeginPortrayed byDeShawn Cavanaugh Colt Cassidyis a student atBayside High. WhenMr. Kimquit abruptly, Zack Morris became their new Government teacher. The other students were fascinated by Zack and his legacy at Bayside. Colt asked him what it was like being King of Bayside and for advice on how they could beat Valley at the Spirit Competition. Zack assured them that he didn't even remember the Spirit Competition from his time at Bayside and they wouldn't remember it once they left either.[1]

characters.name: Cathy Santucci
characters.description: Cathy Santucciwas a student at Bayside High who was really mean toScreechafter he tried to ask her to the senior prom twice. When Cathy tried to bag Screech out to Lisa Turtle, Lisa simply told her that she was a jerk and that the sweater she was wearing clashed with her denim skirt.

characters.name: Chloe
characters.description: ChloeBiographical InformationFull NameChloeResides InCaliforniaOccupationStudentMember of the PRISM ClubEducationBayside HighStatusAliveBackground InformationGenderFemaleEye ColorBrownHair ColorBlackSkin ColorLightSexualityQueerRelationshipsFriendsSpencer BuckleyLexi Haddad-DeFabrizioAisha GarciaLove Interest(s)Aisha Garcia(girlfriend)Series InformationShowsSaved by the Bell (2020)First AppearanceFrom Curse to WorseLatest AppearanceLet the Games BeginProtrayed byAriela Barer Chloeis a student atBayside High. She is datingAisha Garcia. When the students at Bayside learned that a trans girl at another school faced being kicked off the soccer team because other students' parents were suing, Chloe approached Lexi, inviting her to Prism and a sit-in they were having at the school to protest. Lexi said she didn't have time, even when Chloe said that if they don't stand up to hate, it will keep happening. Chloe is described as a cool, queer student who encourages Lexi (Josie Totah) to join the LGBTQ club at Bayside.[6]
characters.personality_section: Chloe is described as a cool, queer student who encourages Lexi (Josie Totah) to join the LGBTQ club at Bayside.[6]

characters.name: Claire
characters.description: ClaireBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnly appearanceWrestling with the FuturePortrayed byValentina Garcia Claireis a doctor who came to theBayside Highcareer fair. Claire, a pediatrician, set up an informational booth at the Bayside career fair to talk to students about her job. While she was there, she witnessed Toddman injuring himself and offered him an ice pack. He accepted it, but then became suspicious she was part of a prank Mac was pulling and left to find Mac. When he found Mac in the library working on the Spirit Competition, he went back and tried to convince Claire to give him another chance, but she ran away from him.

characters.name: Dr. Coleman
characters.description: Dr. ColemanBiographical InformationStatusAliveRelatives'Mrs. Coleman(wife)Nadia Coleman(daughter)AppearancesOnly appearanceThe SubstitutePortrayed byChristopher Guyton Dr. Colemanis Nadia's father. DeVante came to the Coleman house to have dinner with them and meet Nadia's parents. At first, he was nervous, especially after showing up late, but when he commented that they had their own "Geoffrey" when he learned they had a Black British butler, the ice was broken and he started joking with the family. When he told them he didn't want Nadia to drive him home because he didn't want her to have to drive back home by herself afterward, Nadia offered him a Volvo they had that no one was driving. They gave him the key and allowed him to drive it home that night.

characters.name: Mrs. Coleman
characters.description: Mrs. ColemanBiographical InformationStatusAliveRelatives'Dr. Coleman(husband)Nadia Coleman(stepdaughter)AppearancesOnly appearanceThe SubstitutePortrayed byKalilah Harris Mrs. Colemanis Nadia's stepmother. DeVante came to the Coleman house to have dinner with them and meet Nadia's parents. At first, he was nervous, especially after showing up late, but when he commented that they had their own "Geoffrey" when he learned they had a Black British butler, the ice was broken and he started joking with the family. When he told them he didn't want Nadia to drive him home because he didn't want her to have to drive back home by herself afterward, Nadia offered him a Volvo they had that no one was driving. They gave him the key and allowed him to drive it home that night.

characters.name: Nadia Coleman
characters.description: Nadia ColemanBiographical InformationAge15-16(Season 1)16-17(Season 2)StatusAliveRelatives'Mr. Coleman(father)Mrs. Coleman(stepmother)AffiliationsBayside HighInterestDeVante Young(boyfriend)Physical InformationHair colorBlackEye colorBrownAppearancesFirst appearanceThe Fabulous Birchwood BoysPortrayed byMariah Iman Wilson Nadia ColemanisDeVante Young's girlfriend. Nadia is a student atBayside High. She is on Student Council as the Class Treasurer. At the end of the pandemic, Nadia returned to school along with all the other students. Soon afterward, Aisha started to treat Nadia poorly. She said it was because Nadia ghosted DeVante, her friend. Nadia said that she didn't ghost him. They broke up because he cheated on her. Aisha didn't believe that, but Nadia said he kept getting messages from a girl whose number wasn't saved in his phone, sending him emojis. Then she never heard from DeVante again. Aisha later found out DeVante had been texting Toddman and ended up staying with him during the pandemic because he was worried about getting his grandmother sick.[1] TBA

characters.name: Phil Dapatone
characters.description: Phil DapatoneBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnly appearanceThe GiftPortrayed byEddie Pepitone Phil Dapatoneis a record executive who had DeVante audition for him. Phil was at The Max when he gave DeVante his card and left. He was a record executive and wanted DeVante to audition for him. After DeVante finished singing, Phil applauded and asked DeVante to wait to sing again for Chuck. DeVante said he couldn't because he had to get back to school. Phil was disappointed to learn that DeVante was still in school, saying it wouldn't work. He offered to buy DeVante a book of brain teasers if he was so into learning, but advised DeVante not to give up his shot. DeVante decided to go back to school anyway.

characters.name: Principal Davis
characters.description: Principal DavisBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesFirst appearancePilotLast appearanceFrom Curse to WorsePortrayed byRodney J. Hobbs Principal Davisis the former principal of Douglas High. Toddman offered Davis a job at Bayside teaching Modern Southern California History. He accepted the job and on the first day, assigned the class to a group presentation, putting Jamie, DeVante, and Mac together to do a report on earthquakes. When they did their presentation, they got a standing ovation, which surprised Davis.[1]

characters.name: Delores
characters.description: DeloresBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnly appearanceThe SubstitutePortrayed byJill Basey Deloresis Zack and Kelly's maid. Delores was cleaning the Morris house, which interrupted Zack and Mac's green screen surfing. She was oblivious to this interruption.

characters.name: Grandma Doris
characters.description: Grandma DorisBiographical InformationStatusAliveRelatives'DeVante Young(grandson)AppearancesFirst appearanceThe Bayside TriangleLast appearanceThe GiftPortrayed byHilda Boulware Grandma Dorisis DeVante's grandmother. When DeVante was invited to audition forPhil Dapatone, a record executive, Lexie encouraged him to do it, saying once he got successful and rich, he could buy his grandmother a mansion.[1]

characters.name: Frank Douglas
characters.description: Frank DouglasBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnly appearanceWrestling with the FuturePortrayed byStephen O'Mahoney Frank Douglasis a pilot who came toBayside High'scareer fair. Frank, a pilot, came to Bayside's career fair to talk about his job. DeVante was interested because he enjoyed the view of the sky he saw on video game flight simulators. Frank said that games like that were what made him want to be a pilot.

characters.name: Leslie Eisen
characters.description: Leslie EisenBiographical InformationAge32StatusAliveRelatives'Jessie Spano(Step-Daughter)Jamie Spano(Step-Grandson)InterestDavid Spano(Married)Physical InformationGenderFemaleHair colorBlondeEye colorBrownHeight5'7AppearancesFirst appearancePalm Springs Weekend (1)Portrayed byBarbra Brighton  Not much is known about her early life except that she worked at a gym at Palm Springs where she became the personal instructor to Jessie's father David, fell in love with him and accepted his marriage proposal. Leslie is kind and thoughtful, who loved Jessie's father deeply and played along when Zack made a fool of himself to impress her. She is also very forgiving after Jessie became jealous and rejected her attempts to befriend her.
characters.personality_section: Leslie is kind and thoughtful, who loved Jessie's father deeply and played along when Zack made a fool of himself to impress her. She is also very forgiving after Jessie became jealous and rejected her attempts to befriend her.

characters.name: Eric Tramer
characters.description: Eric TramerBiographical InformationAgeBetween 15 and 16Relatives'Mr. Tramer (Father)Unnamed MotherMrs. Spano (Stepmother)Jessie Spano(Stepsister)Unnamed AuntInterestLisa Turtle(dating)Physical InformationHair colorBrownEye colorBrownAppearancesFirst appearanceThe Wicked Stepbrother (Part 1)Last appearanceThe Wicked Stepbrother (Part 2)Portrayed byJosh Hoffman Eric TramerisJessie Spano's obnoxious stepbrother whose only appearance was in a two-part episode in the series. Not much is known about Eric's past except that he lived with his father in New York (what became of his mother has never been mentioned). At his school, he was a prankster and class troublemaker. His father met Jessie's mother and they married with the family moving to her house in California.

characters.name: Aisha Garcia
characters.description: Aisha GarciaSeason 2Season 1Biographical InformationAge15-16(season 1)16-17(season 2)StatusAliveAffiliationsBayside HighInterestChloe(girlfriend)Jamie Spano(ex-boyfriend)Physical InformationGenderFemaleHair colorBlackEye colorBrownHeight5'4"AppearancesFirst appearancePilotPortrayed byAlycia Pascual-Peña Aisha Garciais one of the main characters inSaved by the Bell (2020). She is a transfer from Douglas High and best friend ofDaisy Jiménez. Aisha upset the dynamic upon her arrival to Bayside, but eventually engaged in an intimate relationship with Bayside's most popular guy,Jamie Spano. She is fun-loving but ultracompetitive best friendDaisy Jiménezand Bayside High's new starting quarterback.

She is a transfer from Douglas High and best friend ofDaisy Jiménez. Aisha upset the dynamic upon her arrival to Bayside, but eventually engaged in an intimate relationship with Bayside's most popular guy,Jamie Spano. She is fun-loving but ultracompetitive best friendDaisy Jiménezand Bayside High's new starting quarterback.
characters.personality_section: She is fun-loving but ultracompetitive best friendDaisy Jiménezand Bayside High's new starting quarterback.

characters.name: Philip Tyberius Gorpleman
characters.description: Philip Tyberius GorplemanBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnly appearanceLa Guerra de AishaPortrayed byWilliam McMullen Philip Tyberius Gorplemanis a student atBayside High. Philip was at school when Slater tried to use him to prove to Aisha that no one in high school knew who they were. Unfortunately, Philip revealed himself to be self-assured, knowing that he wanted to grow up to be an ecoterrorist.

characters.name: Greg
characters.description: GregBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesFirst appearanceShowdownLast appearanceThe GiftPortrayed byBrandon Marcel Gregis a student atBayside High. When he overheard DeVante on the bus telling Aisha and Daisy about how their economic status separated them from the other Bayside students, Greg poked his head over the seat to tell them that when he broke his arm, he didn't tell his parents for a week because he was worried about how much it would cost.[1]
